---
title: "LRPGF01: Gain factors for word reading (W)"

date: last-modified
date-format: "YYYY-MM-DD HH:mm:ss Z"

format:
  html:
    code-fold: true
    theme: cosmo
    number-sections: true
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    code-links:
      - repo
---

::: {.callout-note}
Drafted by a LLM-based AI tool (Claude Code/Opus 4.8).
:::

::: {.callout-warning title="Warning: Preliminary model output"}

This model output is preliminary and likely to change as the model evolves and further data are received.

If the model was not fitted in "reporting" mode, the results will be more approximate than where additional sampling has been performed.

:::

```{python}
# | echo: false
import json
from pathlib import Path

import pandas as pd

_here = Path(".")
with open(_here / "config.json") as f:
    config = json.load(f)
factor_summary = pd.read_csv(_here / "factor_summary.csv")
treatment_marginal = pd.read_csv(_here / "treatment_marginal.csv")
rope_summary = pd.read_csv(_here / "rope_summary.csv")
```

## Overview

LRPGF01 is a **gain-factors** model for word reading (`ewrswr`): what is associated
with how much children *gain* across the three period transitions
(t1 $\rightarrow$ t2, t2 $\rightarrow$ t3, t3 $\rightarrow$ t4), modelled as an
ANCOVA — the period post-count given its pre-count — on the logit scale with a
Beta-Binomial likelihood and a child random intercept.

::: {.callout-important title="Causal labelling (DAG guardrails)"}

Under the locked study DAG (`notes/202606231600-dag-revision-consolidated.md`),
**only the randomised on-intervention term is causal** — its coefficient is
identified by the period-1 randomised contrast and reproduces the ITT effect.
Every other coefficient (own baseline, age, cognitive ability, the upstream skills
L and R, and all interactions) is an **adjusted association** confounded by latent
general ability (GA); the child random intercept repairs time-invariant GA up to
shrinkage, so same-wave predictors are "go-together" associations. Report these as
"associated with", **never** "drives". Intervention sessions / dose are never
conditioned on (a DAG collider). SES is excluded: it is not a DAG node and was
found statistically redundant.

:::

**DAG factor set:** on-intervention (causal) · own baseline · age · cognitive
ability (block design) · letter sounds (L) · receptive vocabulary (R) · child
random intercept. **Focal interactions:** group $\times$ ability,
group $\times$ own-baseline, age $\times$ ability.

## Model

$$
\operatorname{logit}\mu_{i,p} = \alpha + \alpha_{\text{period}[p]}
  + \beta_{\text{trt}}\,\text{OnIntervention}_{i,p}
  + \gamma_{\text{own}}\operatorname{logit}_*(W_{\text{pre}})
  + \gamma_A A_{\text{std}}
  + \gamma_{\text{ability}}\,z(\text{blocks})
  + \sum_{s\in\{L,R\}}\gamma_s\operatorname{logit}_*(s_{\text{pre}})
  + \textstyle\sum\text{interactions}
  + u_{\text{child}[i]},
$$

with $W_{\text{post}} \sim \operatorname{BetaBinomial}(N, \mu\kappa, (1-\mu)\kappa)$,
$N = 79$, and $u_{\text{child}} \sim \operatorname{Normal}(0, \sigma_{\text{child}})$.
`OnIntervention` is the immediate arm from period 1 and the waitlist from period 2
(post-crossover). Priors follow the shared template in `priors.py`.

![Model diagram](model_graph.svg){#fig-model .lightbox fig-align="left"}

## Priors

![alpha](prior_alpha.svg){width=24%}
![tau (treatment / group)](prior_tau.svg){width=24%}
![gamma_own](prior_gamma_own.svg){width=24%}
![gamma_age](prior_gamma_age.svg){width=24%}
![gamma_cross (ability / skills / interactions)](prior_gamma_cross.svg){width=24%}
![kappa](prior_kappa.svg){width=24%}

## Prior-predictive check

The prior-predictive distribution of the word-reading count (blue) against the
observed counts (red): the priors should cover the observed range without
concentrating implausibly.

![Prior predictive](prior_predictive_check.png){.lightbox}

## Sampling diagnostics

```{python}
# | echo: false
diag = pd.read_csv(_here / "diagnostics.csv", index_col=0)
diag
```

![Trace](trace_plot.png){.lightbox}

![Energy](energy_plot.png){.lightbox}

![Posterior](posterior_plot.png){.lightbox}

## Posterior-predictive check

![Posterior predictive](posterior_predictive_check.png){.lightbox}

## Findings

### Factor summary

```{python}
# | echo: false
factor_summary
```

- `role`: **causal** = the randomised on-intervention term (ITT); **association** =
  adjusted associations under the DAG (confounded by GA — read as "associated
  with", never "drives").
- `mean` / `lo` / `hi`: posterior mean and equal-tailed 95% credible interval
  (logit scale). `prob_positive` = $P(\text{coef} > 0)$.

The **on-intervention** coefficient (`beta_trt`) is the causal ITT read; identified
by the period-1 randomised contrast, it should reproduce the single-outcome ITT
$\tau$ for word reading ($\approx +0.35$ logit). The focal interactions
(group $\times$ ability, group $\times$ own-baseline, age $\times$ ability) are
expected to be **weakly identified** (wide intervals) at $n\approx54$.

### Items-scale treatment effect

```{python}
# | echo: false
treatment_marginal
```

Average marginal effect of being on intervention, on the probability scale
(`trt_prob_*`) and the items scale (`trt_items_*` = expected additional words read
out of 79), averaged over the fitted sample per posterior draw. The point estimate
is the **median** (transformation-invariant across the logit and items scales);
`prob_trt_pos` = $P(\beta_{\text{trt}} > 0)$ on the logit scale.

### Reporting: direction, magnitude, and practical significance

This follows `notes/202606261304-evidence-strength-and-rope-reporting.md` and the
`METHODS.md` "Interpret" rule, applied here to the model's **one causal term**, the
randomised on-intervention effect (`beta_trt`): report the **median** effect with
intervals, and separate **direction** (is there a benefit?) from **magnitude** (is
it big enough to matter?). Magnitude is judged against a minimally-important
difference $\delta$ — a region of practical equivalence (ROPE) on the items scale —
set per outcome in `measures.ROPE_DELTA` (provisional, owned by the education lead).
The non-randomised factor-summary coefficients above are adjusted associations and
do **not** receive this causal report.

```{python}
# | echo: false
# | output: asis
r = rope_summary.iloc[0]


from language_reading_predictors.statistical_models.reporting import odds_string as _odds

print(
    f"Being on intervention raised word reading by a median of "
    f"**{r.items_median:+.1f} items** "
    f"(50% CrI {r.items_lo50:+.1f} to {r.items_hi50:+.1f}; "
    f"95% CrI {r.items_lo:+.1f} to {r.items_hi:+.1f}). "
    f"**Direction** — evidence it helps: "
    f"pd = {r.pd:.3f} ({_odds(r.pd)}, *{r.direction_label} evidence*). "
    f"**Magnitude** — evidence the benefit is at least "
    f"$\\delta$ = {r.delta_items:g} items: "
    f"P = {r.prob_benefit_ge_delta:.3f} "
    f"({_odds(r.prob_benefit_ge_delta)}, *{r.benefit_label} evidence*); "
    f"probability practically negligible (inside the ROPE): {r.prob_in_rope:.3f}."
)
```

```{python}
# | echo: false
rope_summary.T
```

![ROPE-anchored posterior of the on-intervention effect, and the probability of a benefit of at least delta items](rope_summary.png){.lightbox}

::: {.callout-note title="Reading the evidence labels"}

The round-odds labels (*inconclusive / suggestive / moderate / strong / very
strong*) qualify the **evidence**, never the effect *size*, and are run separately
for the direction and the meaningful-benefit claims. They are a last-resort handle
for prose — the odds and probabilities are the real quantities. At this study's
sample size the point estimate is, on average, magnitude-inflated (a Type-M /
winner's-curse effect), so lead with the interval, not the point.

:::

## LOO-PSIS

```{python}
# | echo: false
# | output: asis
print("```\n" + open(_here / "loo.txt").read() + "\n```")
```

## Configuration

```{python}
# | echo: false
config
```
